intent: Propose an exact primitive decomposition and a G1 review dossier for the single framed question, while identifying benchmark changes, normalizations, kill conditions, and unresolved scope risks without confirming G1.

entity.title: Primitive graph for consumable quality certificates
entity.summary: A proposed decomposition of actors, states, timing, information, actions, payoffs, equilibrium objects, outcomes, benchmark perturbations, normalizations, and scope costs for the exact framed question.

nodes.label: Two long-lived sellers
nodes.economic_meaning: Two ex ante identical sellers make repeated issuance choices and internalize how current certificates and depletion affect discounted future successful-trade margins.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Exactly two sellers persist across periods.
Seller identities are payoff-symmetric before private capacity draws.

nodes.label: Sequence of short-lived buyers
nodes.economic_meaning: One new buyer acts per period and has no continuation payoff, so inspection and seller choice are static best responses to the current public state and beliefs.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Exactly one buyer arrives in each period.
Each buyer exits the environment after that period.

nodes.label: Short-lived private capacity
nodes.economic_meaning: An uncertified seller draws H or L with probability p and 1-p; H succeeds if traded and L fails, while untraded uncertified capacity expires at period end.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: For a seller without an outstanding certificate, theta is independently H with p in (0,1).
Draws are independent across sellers and periods in the proposed scope.

nodes.label: Binary public certificate stock
nodes.economic_meaning: Each seller can hold at most one visible truthful claim to a reserved high-capacity service unit; this stock is the intertemporal information inventory.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: s_i is in {0,1} and publicly observed after issuance.
An outstanding certificate guarantees successful certified service.

nodes.label: Costly truthful issuance
nodes.economic_meaning: A currently uncertified H seller may pay k to convert its current high unit into one public certificate; L sellers and already certified sellers cannot issue.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Certification is feasible only when theta_i=H and s_i=0.
The issuance cost is k and is paid when the certificate is created.

nodes.label: Carryover and trade-triggered depletion
nodes.economic_meaning: An untraded certificate survives, whereas a certified trade consumes it; buyer allocation therefore controls the transition of the public certificate stock.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: If a certified seller is chosen, its next-period stock is zero.
If a certified seller is not chosen, its certificate carries to the next period.

nodes.label: Public stocks and private current capacity
nodes.economic_meaning: The buyer observes certificate stocks but not the current capacity of an uncertified seller; each seller observes its own current capacity before issuance.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Post-issuance s=(s_1,s_2) is public.
Uncertified theta_i remains private unless inspected.

nodes.label: Costly inspection of one uncertified seller
nodes.economic_meaning: The buyer may pay c to reveal the current capacity of exactly one eligible uncertified seller before making its final trade or exit choice.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: c is in (0,1).
At most one uncertified seller can be inspected in a period.

nodes.label: Buy, redirect after inspection, or exit
nodes.economic_meaning: After any inspection result, the buyer chooses one seller or exits; this choice determines current success and, for a certified seller, future certificate depletion.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: The buyer can buy without inspecting or exit.
After inspection the buyer may choose either seller or exit.

nodes.label: Buyer service value and search cost
nodes.economic_meaning: Buyer payoff is one for successful service, zero for unsuccessful service or exit, minus c if inspection was used.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Service value is normalized to one.
No other buyer transfer is included in the proposed scope.

nodes.label: Successful-trade margin and discounting
nodes.economic_meaning: A chosen seller earns m only when service succeeds, pays any current issuance cost k, and discounts continuation profit by delta.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: m is positive and delta is in (0,1).
An unsuccessful trade yields no seller margin.

nodes.label: State, draw, issuance, buyer action, service, transition
nodes.economic_meaning: The within-period order makes issuance informative before inspection and makes buyer allocation causally prior to certificate depletion and the next public state.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Observe inherited stock; draw uncertified capacities; choose issuance; observe post-issuance stock; inspect or choose; realize service; update stock.
Untraded uncertified capacity expires after the transition.

nodes.label: Composition of the uncertified pool
nodes.economic_meaning: Seller issuance selection and prior certificate survival jointly determine the posterior probability that an uncertified seller is currently H in each public state.

nodes.label: State-contingent return to inspection
nodes.economic_meaning: The value of inspecting is the improvement in the buyer's allocation or exit choice after learning one uncertified capacity, net of c, conditional on the public stock and induced pool composition.

nodes.label: Symmetric stationary Markov perfect equilibrium candidates
nodes.economic_meaning: Sellers use stationary Markov issuance policies and buyers use state-contingent inspection and allocation best responses; mixed strategies are allowed and multiplicity is retained.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Strategies depend only on payoff-relevant public stock, private current capacity where applicable, and current inspection information.
No equilibrium branch is selected by combining points from distinct connected branches.

nodes.label: Endogenous stationary state distribution
nodes.economic_meaning: Issuance, buyer allocation, carryover, and depletion induce a stationary distribution over public certificate stocks and current uncertified composition.

nodes.label: Search, service success, and buyer surplus
nodes.economic_meaning: Q is the stationary inspection probability, A is successful service per arriving buyer with exit counted as zero, and U is expected service value minus inspection cost.

nodes.label: Ordered certification-cost triple
nodes.economic_meaning: Hold p, c, m, delta and institutional primitives fixed while comparing 0 <= k_L < k_M < k_H <= m along one connected equilibrium branch.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Only k changes within a full-model comparative-static exercise.
Equilibrium policies and the stationary distribution may respond to k.

nodes.label: Strict interior reversal statistic
nodes.economic_meaning: For Y in {Q,A,U}, a negative product (Y(k_M)-Y(k_L)) times (Y(k_H)-Y(k_M)) is the exact falsifier of weak monotonicity on the selected cost triple.

nodes.label: Flow-only benchmark delta
nodes.economic_meaning: Relative to the full model, reset stocks each period and expire every current certificate after the buyer stage; retain current truthful issuance, its cost k, endogenous inspection, and all other primitives.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Changed primitives: certificate carryover and the intertemporal stock transition only.
Unchanged primitives: actors, capacity draws, issuance eligibility, k, c, m, delta, information within the period, actions, payoffs, and equilibrium optimization.

nodes.label: Matched exogenous-expiry benchmark delta
nodes.economic_meaning: Relative to the full model, suppress automatic consumption after certified trade and instead expire each outstanding certificate with a choice-independent hazard h_0 calibrated to the paired full branch at k_M and fixed across the cost triple.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Changed primitive: the dependence of certificate depletion on buyer trade.
Matched derived object: the reference conditional depletion probability at k_M.
Unchanged primitives: binary stock, issuance eligibility and cost, buyer inspection and allocation actions, payoffs, and all nondepletion timing.

nodes.label: Fixed-inspection benchmark delta
nodes.economic_meaning: Relative to the full model, impose the branch's k_M inspection probability and target lottery in every public state at all three costs while retaining endogenous issuance, stock, trade-triggered depletion, stationary state composition, and conditional purchase or exit responses.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Changed primitive: the buyer's ability to reoptimize the state-contingent inspection and target-selection rule as k changes.
Unchanged primitives: all certificate laws, seller choices, conditional buyer allocation choices, payoffs, and capacity process.

nodes.label: Payoff units, seller labels, and tie-breaking normalizations
nodes.economic_meaning: Buyer service value one fixes utility units, seller labels have no primitive asymmetry, and uniform randomization resolves payoff ties; these conventions are not channel perturbations.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: Changing monetary units rescales 1, c, k, and m together without changing choices.
Relabeling sellers leaves primitives invariant.
Uniform tie-breaking applies only at exact indifference.

nodes.label: Scope risk: meaning of an outstanding certificate
nodes.economic_meaning: The proposed scope treats a certificate as a reserved high-capacity unit and postpones a new usable capacity draw until depletion; the original seed does not independently specify how fresh capacity interacts with an outstanding certificate.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: G1 must accept, revise, or reject the reserved-unit interpretation before formalization.

nodes.label: Scope risk: buyer payment versus seller margin
nodes.economic_meaning: The proposed buyer payoff contains service value and search cost but no purchase price, while m is an exogenous seller margin; adding a transfer or price would change exit and inspection incentives.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: G1 must confirm whether the no-price buyer payoff and fixed seller margin are intended primitives or placeholders.

nodes.label: Scope risk: iid capacity and branch matching
nodes.economic_meaning: Independence of capacity draws and connected-branch comparisons make the question exact but exclude persistent capacity and leave cross-benchmark branch pairing as a formal implementation choice.
primitive_sufficient_conditions: G1 must decide whether iid capacity is core scope.
A later formal route must specify an operational homotopy or correspondence rule for pairing benchmark branches without splicing equilibria.

edges.economic_meaning: Only a current H draw makes truthful issuance feasible.
Issuance converts a current high unit into a public stock at cost k.
The public stock reveals guaranteed service while uncertified capacity remains hidden.
Public stock and beliefs determine what information inspection can add.
Inspection changes the buyer's information before seller choice or exit.
Choosing a certified seller consumes its certificate; choosing elsewhere preserves it.
Trade-linked depletion is part of the transition kernel over public stocks.
Selective issuance removes some H draws from the uncertified pool.
Outstanding stocks determine which sellers enter the current uncertified drawing and selection process.
The posterior quality of uncertified sellers determines the information value of inspection.
Service value and c convert information into a net inspection return.
The buyer inspects only as prescribed by its state-contingent best response or mixing condition.
The current cost, successful-trade margin, and continuation value determine issuance incentives.
The information and action order defines admissible strategies and best responses.
An equilibrium policy profile induces the transition kernel whose invariant distribution is used.
Q, A, and U aggregate state-contingent behavior under the stationary distribution.
Changing k directly changes the seller's issuance cost while allowing equilibrium feedback.
The three cost-indexed outcome levels enter the exact strict-reversal product.
The flow benchmark deletes intertemporal carryover rather than changing current issuance or inspection.
The matched-expiry benchmark replaces choice-dependent depletion with a fixed matched hazard.
The fixed-inspection benchmark removes reoptimization of the state-contingent inspection rule but retains compositional changes in public states.
Unit service value fixes scale but is not a benchmark channel.

entity.title: G1 dossier for the consumable-certificate question and benchmarks
entity.summary: A proposed G1 review packet that records exact supporting objects, economic importance, kill conditions, benchmark separation, and unresolved scope risks without making a gate decision.

requirements.description: The exact question fixes the full environment, branch-specific cost comparison, outcomes Q/A/U, and the strict negative-product test, while leaving every direction unresolved.

The proposed economic consequence is whether certification-cost comparisons miss an information-inventory effect operating through the stationary certificate stock, the residual uncertified pool, and the option value of inspection; no policy or welfare sign is claimed.

The channel is killed if no admissible primitive vector, connected equilibrium branch, and ordered cost triple yields the strict reversal together with the benchmark channel tests; a reversal reproduced by the flow-only model or unchanged under matched exogenous expiry does not establish the consumable-stock explanation.

The flow-only delta changes carryover, the matched-expiry delta changes the dependence of depletion on buyer choice while matching the reference hazard, and the fixed-inspection delta changes only reoptimization of the state-contingent inspection rule; all unchanged primitives and normalizations are named.

Human review must resolve whether an outstanding certificate reserves a high unit, whether buyer payoff excludes a purchase price while seller margin is fixed, whether iid capacity is core, and how equilibrium branches are paired across benchmarks. These choices can change the mechanism or its test.
The question, benchmark set, primitive graph, and this dossier remain agent proposals. No G1 promotion, scope confirmation, or structural decision exists until a human records it.

payload.rationale: The question is falsifiable and the channel benchmarks are exact enough for review, but the reserved-unit interpretation, payoff-transfer convention, iid-capacity restriction, and cross-benchmark branch-pairing rule are economically consequential scope choices. The dossier therefore proposes human revision or explicit acceptance of those items before any G1 promotion; this is not a gate decision.

outcome.rationale: Proposes one exact primitive graph and one G1 dossier for the existing research question and benchmark set, with benchmark changes, normalizations, kill conditions, and scope risks disclosed; no G1 decision or approval is made.
